No G1 (no named counterparty) or G2 (no concrete external commitment).
edge: M — dated forward binary on a real first-efficacy signal, and price ($26.99) sits below the 52w high ($40.65) so efficacy is not fully re-rated; but offset by single early sentinel cohort (~6 pts, n too small for robust signal), efficacy entirely unproven, and "expected" timing not a hard guided date.
analysis: TYRA's BEACH301 Phase 2 sentinel-cohort readout is a genuine dated, forward binary: initial 6-month average height-velocity and safety data for oral dabogratinib in pediatric achondroplasia is expected Q4 2026 (refined from "2H26" in the May 2026 Q1 report), and all four dose levels have now cleared with no notable safety events, so this is purely an efficacy event. It satisfies Bloom G3 as a specific unpriced figure — the first human efficacy signal for the only oral FGFR3 inhibitor in the class — and is not a macro bet. It stays WATCH not ACTIVE because it is a single early readout in a tiny sentinel cohort with unproven efficacy and soft "expected" timing; cash runway into 2H 2028 ($383.5M as of 3/31/26) removes near-term financing risk but adds no catalyst urgency.

bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Fluidstack + Google $1.4B backstop) and G2 (concrete signed commitment, ~$9.3B total contracted revenue / ~$669M avg annual NOI). G3 FAILS — the contracted figure is the centerpiece of every analyst model, not currently unpriced.
edge: L — deal signed Sept 2025 and already heavily re-rated; "deliver on time" is the consensus base case, not a hidden edge.
analysis: The Fluidstack milestone is real, named, and dated (G1/G2 met), but it is not a clean unpriced arb: the deal fired Sept 25, 2025 and CIFR has already re-rated ~7x off its 52-wk low ($3.29) to ~$24.50 (up 8% today, near 52-wk high $28.62, vs $16.82 200-day avg). Management narrates the milestone every quarter (Q1 2026 call: on track, 99% procured, building topped out) and analysts model the ~$9.3B contracted revenue explicitly (median PT $26.50, MS Overweight $48.50), so G3 fails — there is no currently-unpriced figure. This is an AI-datacenter execution/momentum bet whose base case is already in the price; the only forward optionality is a binary on-time-vs-slip outcome, which caps it at a low-conviction WATCH.
primary_source: Cipher Mining Q1 2026 earnings call (Motley Fool transcript, 2026-05-05) confirming Barber Lake "tracking to meet contractual early access and rack ready dates," 99% equipment procured; original 168MW/$3B Fluidstack + Google deal GlobeNewswire 2025-09-25; Morgan Stanley PT $48.50 / Bernstein $32 initiation, June 2026.

catalyst: UK High Court sanction hearing on the Lilly scheme of arrangement ($38 cash + up to $9 CVR), the last gate before the deal closes.
dated_milestone: UK High Court sanction hearing 6/22/2026; scheme effective shortly after, expected close Q3'26
bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Eli Lilly) + G2 (concrete binding scheme of arrangement, $38 cash + up to $9 CVR)
edge: L
analysis: Every claim in the triage flag verifies: Lilly's $38 cash + up to $9 CVR scheme was approved by Centessa holders 6/12/2026 (126.6M for vs 23k against), HSR waiting period expired 5/21/2026, and the only remaining condition is the UK High Court sanction hearing 6/22/2026 with close expected Q3'26 — a genuine, dated, forward catalyst (G1 named Lilly, G2 binding scheme). However the arb is effectively FIRED: CNTA trades $39.72, $1.72 ABOVE the $38.00 cash floor, so there is no positive spread to the cash consideration and the deal is fully de-risked into the sanction hearing. The remaining upside is purely the CVR, a multi-year binary on FDA approvals of cleminorexton/ORX142 (NT2/IH through 2030) — speculative optionality, not a clean late-stage cash arb; downside if the deal somehow broke is to ~$38 or below, an asymmetric loss vs ~$1.72 of capped CVR optionality, so WATCH/score 4 rather than ACTIVE.
primary_source: Centessa/Lilly 8-K + DEFM14A; HSR expired 5/21/2026 (TradingView/Globe and Mail), shareholder approval 6/12/2026 (StockTitan 8-K, TipRanks), court sanction 6/22/2026 — accessed 2026-06-13

catalyst: Dana signed a definitive Reverse Morris Trust to combine with Eaton's Mobility business (~$5.1B equity / ~$10B EV), with Eaton holders owning >=50.1% and Dana ~49.9% of NewCo, pending a Dana shareholder vote and Q1 2027 close.
dated_milestone: Dana shareholder vote (date TBD, not yet scheduled) -> expected close Q1 2027
bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Eaton Mobility) + G2 (concrete commitment: RMT terms, ~$1.1B cash to Eaton, $250M synergies, ~1.2x net lev); G3 weak/absent
edge: L - no merger-arb spread exists (stock-for-stock RMT, Dana holders roll into 49.9% of NewCo, no fixed cash takeout); deal already re-rated (DAN -10% on Jun 11, Barclays flags ~$1.5B overpay), so upside is a long-dated 2027-2030 synergy/margin story, not a discrete unpriced near-term catalyst.
analysis: The catalyst is real, specific and dated: Dana confirmed a definitive RMT with Eaton's Mobility business on June 11, 2026, with a Dana shareholder vote pending and close targeted for Q1 2027 (forward, not fired). But it fails the catalyst-arb premise of the lane — an RMT is a stock-for-stock combination with no per-share cash consideration to Dana holders, so there is no clean spread to capture, and the equity already re-rated sharply on announcement (-10%, analysts calling the price dilutive). The remaining upside is an integration/synergy thesis playing out into 2030, which is a fundamental view rather than a discrete unpriced forward catalyst; demote to WATCH pending a hard, dated vote/proxy milestone.
primary_source: Dana press release "Announces Agreement to Combine with Eaton's Mobility Business" (Jun 11, 2026), dana.com; corroborated by Bloomberg/Yahoo Finance & Benzinga coverage (Jun 11, 2026)

catalyst: Alpha Tau is running a modular FDA PMA for Alpha DaRT in recurrent cSCC plus an expanded REGAIN glioblastoma feasibility study, with multiple soft-dated 2026 readouts but no single near-term hard FDA approval decision.
dated_milestone: ReSTART (cSCC) pivotal top-line data and REGAIN glioblastoma enrollment-completion/additional data, both guided to "around the end of 2026" (next concrete date: REGAIN enrollment completion H2 2026); PMA submission is modular with no committed completion or FDA decision date.
bloom_gates: Weak G3 only — a specific dated figure (ReSTART top-line cSCC data ~end-2026) exists, but it is a data readout rather than a hard binary approval, the date is soft ("around"), and the REGAIN 100% local-control interim is already disclosed (only 3 patients) so partly priced. No named G1 counterparty, no G2 contractual commitment.
edge: L — REGAIN interim (100% local control, 67% CR in 3 pts) is already public and ReSTART enrollment completed May 2026, so the bull thesis is partly re-rated; stock is up ~3.5x off the 52w low ($2.85→$9.85, 50d $8.84 vs 200d $6.21), indicating momentum already reflects the regulatory optimism.
analysis: Modular PMA = no single hard PDUFA-style decision; the only forward dated items are soft "around end of 2026" data readouts (ReSTART cSCC top-line, REGAIN GBM additional data) plus REGAIN enrollment completion in H2 2026, and the headline 100%-local-control GBM signal is a 3-patient interim that is already disclosed and partly priced. This is a dated but diffuse multi-milestone story, not a near-term binary approval, so it stays WATCH rather than ACTIVE. Cash $80.2M as of Mar 31 2026 with ~$23M quarterly burn is a financing-risk overhang ahead of those readouts.
primary_source: Alpha Tau Q1 2026 results / corporate update, GlobeNewswire 2026-05-18 (cSCC PMA first module Jan 5 2026; ReSTART top-line and REGAIN data "around end of 2026"; REGAIN enrollment H2 2026; $80.2M cash); REGAIN FDA expansion clearance via RTTNews/Dermatology Times 2026.

catalyst: EQR is the larger-share side of an all-stock merger-of-equals with AvalonBay (fixed 2.793 EQR-per-AVB ratio, ~$69B EV) announced May 20 2026, pending both shareholder votes and closing in 2H26.
dated_milestone: EQR shareholder vote at June 18, 2026 annual meeting (voting deadline June 17); deal close expected 2H26.
instrument: EQR common (NYSE); true arb leg is long AVB / short EQR at 2.793x, not EQR outright.
bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty AvalonBay), G2 (concrete fixed exchange ratio 2.793x / 51.2-48.8% ownership split), G3 (dated June 18 2026 vote)
edge: L — all-stock MOE at fixed ratio, no cash/collar; EQR is the acquirer-equivalent side so it carries no capturable target premium. Residual spread (AVB $187.03 vs implied 2.793x67.34=$188.09 = ~0.57% gross) accrues to the long-AVB/short-EQR leg, is thin and largely priced with the vote ~5 days out. EQR alone is a directional REIT bet, not an arb.
analysis: The catalyst is real, named, dated and forward — it clears G1/G2/G3 and is not yet fired (votes pending, close 2H26). But for the flagged instrument (EQR) specifically there is no merger-arb edge: this is a stock-for-stock merger-of-equals at a fixed ratio with no cash and no collar, and EQR sits on the larger-ownership (acquirer-equivalent) side, so holding EQR captures no deal premium and simply rolls into the combined company. Any residual spread (~0.57% gross) belongs to the long-AVB/short-EQR pair and is razor-thin with the vote ~5 days away; downgrade to WATCH because the event is genuine but the EQR-as-arb thesis does not hold.
primary_source: EQR/AVB merger announcement and SEC DEF 14A/DEFA14A/8-K filings (announced May 20-21 2026; EQR meeting June 18 2026) — SEC EDGAR + CNBC, 2026.

dated_milestone: Monotherapy expansion + combination dose-escalation data narrowed to H1 2027 (next date: ~mid-2027); no major remaining 2026 ERAS-0015 data catalyst (mid-May 2026 readout already fired).
bloom_gates: G2 (concrete committed milestone: company-guided H1 2027 expansion/combo readout) + G3 (specific efficacy bar to clear: prior uORR up to 75% post-ICI/platinum NSCLC, 50% PDAC at 24/32mg RDE that durability must confirm)
edge: L — the forward catalyst is ~12 months out (H1 2027), so no near-term timing edge; the imminent mid-May 2026 / ASCO 2026 monotherapy readout has already passed and the positive dose-escalation signal is already priced/re-rated.
analysis: ERAS-0015 is a real, well-defined binary clinical asset: positive preliminary Phase 1 dose-escalation data (uORR up to 75% in post-ICI/platinum NSCLC, 50% PDAC, no DLTs, 24/32mg RDE) was disclosed Jan 12 and reconfirmed/expanded April 27, 2026 — so the de-risking event has already fired and been re-rated. The next genuine value-inflection (monotherapy expansion + combination dose-escalation data) is company-guided to H1 2027, making it distant rather than imminent. Triage flag is correct: keep on WATCH for the H1 2027 readout, but no actionable near-term forward catalyst, so this does not qualify for ACTIVE.
primary_source: Erasca press release "Positive Preliminary Phase 1 Dose Escalation Data for ERAS-0015" (investors.erasca.com, April 27, 2026) + "Promising Early Clinical Data and 2026-2027 Milestones" (globenewswire, Jan 12, 2026); H1 2027 milestone narrowing per Q1 2026 business update.

company_name: Guardian Metal Resources PLC Sponsored ADR
catalyst: Guardian Metal targets an August 2026 Plan of Operations / NEPA filing with the BLM for its 100%-owned Pilot Mountain tungsten project (Nevada), advancing a DPA Title III-funded PFS toward permitting in a critical-mineral supply shortage.
dated_milestone: Plan of Operations submission to BLM targeted August 2026 (next dated step); PFS workstreams ongoing, no PFS completion date published.
bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: U.S. Department of War) + G2 (concrete commitment: $6.2m DPA Title III award to Golden Metal Resources USA LLC). G3 fails — no specific currently-unpriced figure (no PFS NPV/IRR or offtake tonnage/price published yet).
edge: L — DATED forward milestone is real but procedural (a permitting FILING, not a value-crystallizing PFS NPV or offtake); and the triage 'micro-cap' premise is wrong: GMTL is ~$2.54B mkt cap, pre-revenue, 5 employees, IPO'd 2026-03-20, so a strategic-tungsten/DPA premium is already richly priced and the stock already re-rated ~9-17% on the May 6 progress update.
analysis: The DPA Title III commitment is genuine, named and dated (U.S. Department of War, $6.2m, per the May 6 2026 release), and the Aug-2026 BLM Plan-of-Operations filing is a real forward permitting step — passing G1/G2 and the dated/forward tests. But it fails G3: there is no specific unpriced figure to anchor (no completed PFS economics, no named offtake), and the milestone is administrative rather than economic. Decisively, GMTL is not a micro-cap as triaged but a ~$2.54B-cap pre-revenue exploration ADR; at that valuation the critical-mineral premium and permitting optionality already appear priced, leaving thin unpriced edge and material macro/sentiment dependence (tungsten geopolitics).
primary_source: Guardian Metal Resources PLC "Pilot Mountain Pre-Feasibility Progress Update," AccessNewswire / StockTitan, May 6, 2026; FMP quote/profile 2026-06-13 (mktcap $2.54B, IPO 2026-03-20).

bloom_gates: none cleanly — closest is a partial G2 (board-committed, dated distribution mechanics), but no named counterparty (G1) and no specific currently-unpriced SOP figure (G3) surfaced
edge: L — breakup telegraphed since the 2024-25 Elliott-driven plan; HON trades near its 200-day and mid 52-wk range, so the separation is broadly re-rated and the value-discovery event (WI trading) is imminent rather than unpriced
analysis: Catalyst is fully confirmed and hard-dated: Honeywell's board (June 5, 2026 release) set a June 15 record date and June 29 distribution for the tax-free spin of Honeywell Aerospace (>$17B 2025 revenue, ticker HONA) alongside a 1-for-2 reverse split, finishing the three-way breakup. It passes dated + forward + not-a-macro-bet, but fails the strict Bloom test for a fresh, specific, currently-unpriced figure: it is well-telegraphed spin mechanics with no named counterparty or new unpriced SOP delta, and the price already reflects the separation thesis. When-issued trading starts ~June 15, so the discovery event is essentially fired. Holds as WATCH for any post-spin SOP dislocation, not an ACTIVE fire.
primary_source: Honeywell PRNewswire / honeywell.com press release "Honeywell Board of Directors Sets Record Date..." dated June 5, 2026; corroborated by Honeywell Aerospace Form 10-12B/A (SEC, FY2026)

Downgrade from ACTIVE/8 to WATCH/4: genuine clean spread but the edge is largely gone, not a fresh forward opportunity.

edge: L — public since Sept 2025 and widely covered/re-rated over ~9 months; no specific unpriced figure and no confirmed near-term hard date.
analysis: The spin is real and structural but materially mis-flagged by triage: KBR has only announced "strategic intent" (Sept 24, 2025), with final board approval, IRS ruling and Form 10 effectiveness still pending, and official guidance targets mid-to-late 2026 — not the 4-Jan-2027 date in the flag, which I could not verify in any KBR release or SEC filing. As a long-public, well-digested separation with no disclosed sum-of-parts/spin-valuation gap to anchor an unpriced figure, it fails the strict FORWARD/unpriced bar for ACTIVE and the Bloom G1/G3 gates. Keep as WATCH and refresh when a Form 10 is filed or a firm record/distribution date is set.
primary_source: KBR press release "Announces Strategic Intent to Spin Off Mission Technology Solutions," GlobeNewswire/KBR IR, Sept 24, 2025; reaffirmed in KBR FY2025/FY2026 10-Q filings (mid-to-late 2026 target).

edge: L — the supply event has already largely fired: stock is $47.83, ~22% below the $61 secondary price and near its 52-wk low ($43.49), well under the 50d ($67.89)/200d ($77.51) MAs, so the overhang is already re-rated; the forward leg (own post-lock-up clearing) is a generic mean-reversion/sentiment bet on a known calendar event, not a discrete unpriced catalyst.
analysis: The dated, named, real supply event is verified — an upsized $854M secondary priced May 28 2026 at $61 (all proceeds to selling stockholders; company got $0), with a 90-day deal lock-up from May 28 (~Aug 26 expiry) and a separate existing lock-up on ~29% of shares ending Jul 18 2026. But the catalyst is effectively fired: the market has already punished the overhang, with the stock at $47.83 versus the $61 deal price and near 52-week lows, so the supply leg is re-rated rather than forward. What remains is a fade-then-own mean-reversion thesis on a fully-telegraphed lock-up calendar with no specific unpriced figure (G3 fails), so it stays a watch-only name rather than an actionable unpriced catalyst.
primary_source: Karman Space & Defense "Announces Pricing of Upsized Secondary Offering" (BusinessWire / 424B7 / StockTitan), May 28 2026; FMP live quote $47.83 (Jun 2026)

But it fails the strict convexity test: NPAC trades $10.36 against a ~$10.30 trust, meaning the deal floor is already priced and there is no specific currently-unpriced figure (G3 fails) — the targeted $150M+ financing is aspirational, not committed. edge: L — the only forward event is a company-controlled regulatory FILING, not a binary; efficacy risk already retired (FDA pre-aligned, UTOPIA pivotal data of 77.8% 3-mo CR / 94.5% 6-mo durability already public and re-rated in May 2026), so the filing itself is low-information and largely anticipated.
analysis: Triage read is correct: lead drug ZUSDURI (UGN-102) is already FDA-approved and launching ($29.2M Q1 2026 revenue, +109% QoQ), so it is fired/re-rated, not a catalyst. The forward event is the UGN-103 NDA submission targeted for Q3 2026 — real, dated, and forward, but it is an administrative filing with binary efficacy risk already removed (FDA agreed on submission strategy; pivotal UTOPIA data already disclosed), and the next true binary (an approval/PDUFA decision) sits in 2027 beyond a near-term window. No named counterparty and no specific currently-unpriced figure, so it does not clear the strict Bloom gate as a hard catalyst.
primary_source: UroGen GlobeNewswire/8-K, "94.5% Six-Month Duration of Response in Phase 3 UTOPIA... heads for H2 2026 NDA filing" (2026-05-15); ZUSDURI launch + Q1 2026 results PR (2026); FDA NDA-strategy agreement PR.

catalyst: VK2735 obesity program: nearest dated event is the Phase 1 subcutaneous maintenance dosing study readout in Q3 2026, while the pivotal Phase 3 VANQUISH-1/-2 obesity topline (the real stock-moving binary) is guided to 2027.
dated_milestone: Phase 1 subcutaneous maintenance dosing study topline — Q3 2026; pivotal VANQUISH-1/-2 topline — 2027 (oral maintenance readout H1 2027)
instrument: equity (VKTX); long-dated calls if playing the 2027 VANQUISH binary
bloom_gates: G3 partial — specific named trial (Phase 1 maintenance, Q3 2026) with a date, but it is a low-magnitude PK/safety study, not the high-asymmetry pivotal binary; the pivotal VANQUISH topline (G3-qualifying obesity binary) lacks a precise sub-quarter date and is ~12 months out in 2027.
edge: L — triage premise was wrong (it labeled a Q3 2026 'dated obesity binary'; the Q3 2026 event is only the Phase 1 maintenance dosing/PK readout, while the real obesity binary, VANQUISH-1/-2, completes in 2027). The near-term catalyst is low-magnitude; the stock-moving one is too far out for an event window. VKTX is a heavily-covered ~large-cap obesity name where asymmetry is not obviously mispriced.
analysis: Verification found the triage flag materially mischaracterized the timeline: VANQUISH-1 (~4,500 pts) and VANQUISH-2 (~1,000 pts) are 78-week pivotal obesity trials with enrollment completed Q4 2025 / Q1 2026, and management guides topline to 2027 — NOT Q3 2026. The only Q3 2026 dated event is the Phase 1 subcutaneous maintenance dosing study (PK/safety/tolerability), a minor de-risking readout that is not a high-asymmetry binary. Net: one genuine near-term dated event exists but is low-edge, the true binary is ~12 months out and not precisely dated, and the name is well-covered/well-priced, so this stays WATCH, not ACTIVE.
primary_source: Viking Therapeutics Q1 2026 earnings call / 8-K (reported May 2026) and VANQUISH-2 enrollment-completion release dated March 26, 2026; corroborated by VANQUISH-1 enrollment release Nov 19, 2025.

catalyst: FDA PDUFA action date Nov 30, 2026 on povetacicept BLA for IgA nephropathy under accelerated approval pathway.
dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date Nov 30, 2026 (BLA accepted Jun 1, 2026)
bloom_gates: G2 (FDA-accepted BLA with assigned action date) — partial; fails G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure)
edge: L — Phase 3 RAINIER win already disclosed, PoS re-rated to ~80%, IgAN risk-adj sales already in $2.1B consensus; accelerated-approval BLA acceptance is procedural, so most value is re-rated and embedded in a $113B mega-cap.
analysis: PDUFA Nov 30, 2026 is real, dated and forward (BLA accepted Jun 1, 2026), so the event itself is concrete. But the asymmetry is weak: the positive Phase 3 RAINIER readout (52% UPCR reduction) is already public, analysts lifted probability of success to ~80% and risk-adjusted IgAN sales consensus to ~$2.1B for 2026, leaving little unpriced upside on a high-probability accelerated approval. At a $113B market cap an incremental nephrology launch is not a mispriced binary — confirms triage as WATCH (event clock, no edge), not an ACTIVE asymmetric bet.
primary_source: Vertex/Businesswire BLA acceptance press release, Jun 1, 2026 (PDUFA Nov 30, 2026); S&P Global Market Intelligence, Apr 2026 ($2.1B risk-adj IgAN sales, ~80% PoS)

catalyst: Definitive all-cash acquisition of Whitestone REIT by Ares Real Estate at $19.00/sh (~$1.7B), board-approved, DEFM14A filed, awaiting shareholder vote with close expected Q3 2026.
dated_milestone: Special shareholder meeting pending (record date May 14 2026; meeting before early Oct 2026), deal close expected Q3 2026.
instrument: WSR common (long for deal close at $19.00, or bump optionality)
bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Ares / AREG Wizard Intermediate LP) + G2 (concrete $19.00/sh all-cash, ~$1.7B definitive merger agreement dated Apr 8 2026)
edge: L — price $19.06 is ABOVE the $19.00 cash terms, so the announced merger-arb spread is negative (-0.3%); no positive carry to capture. The only forward edge is a topping-bid/bump, plausible given a live activist proxy contest (PREC14A/PRRN14A/DFAN14A), but that is speculative, not the dated arb flagged.
analysis: The takeover is real, specific, dated and not yet closed: Ares is acquiring WSR for $19.00/sh all cash, board-approved Apr 8/announced Apr 9 2026, DEFM14A filed, record date May 14, shareholder vote pending and close targeted Q3 2026 (gates G1+G2 met). However WSR trades at $19.06 — six cents ABOVE the cash consideration — so the announced spread is negative and offers no merger-arb edge; the market is implicitly pricing a sweetener, consistent with the ongoing dissident proxy contest. As an announced-spread arb it is effectively re-rated/dead money to the floor, so it does not clear the strict FORWARD-edge bar; downgrade from ACTIVE to WATCH as a bump-optionality name only.
primary_source: WSR DEFM14A / 8-K and GlobeNewswire release "Whitestone REIT to Be Acquired by Ares for $1.7 Billion," Apr 9 2026; FMP quote $19.06 (Jun 13 2026 close)

catalyst: Ziff Davis is running a portfolio break-up: Connectivity sold to Accenture for $1.2B (definitive, Mar-2026) with 2026 guidance withheld pending "additional value-creating transactions," but no next divestiture is named/dated.
dated_milestone: Connectivity-to-Accenture close (outside date Dec 2, 2026; ext. Mar 2, 2027); next read = Q2 2026 earnings ~early Aug 2026. No dated milestone for any additional divestiture.
bloom_gates: G1 only — named counterparty (Accenture, $1.2B definitive). G2 fails (no concrete commitment on the next divestiture — only "exploring"). G3 fails (no disclosed SOP figure / specific unpriced $ for additional asset sales).
edge: L — the only hard leg (Connectivity sale) is already announced and priced; stock at $46.26 near 52w-high $50.55, ~2x off $22.45 low and well above 200d $38.29, so the break-up optionality is largely re-rated. The forward "more divestitures" thesis is undated aspiration.
analysis: The Connectivity/Accenture $1.2B sale is a real, definitive, named transaction (G1) but it is already FIRED (announced Mar 3, 2026) and the stock has re-rated ~2x off its low to near 52-week highs, so the discrete catalyst is largely priced. The forward sum-of-parts break-up claim — additional division sales teed up — has no named counterparty, no committed timeline, and no disclosed dollar/SOP figure (G2 and G3 both fail), making it aspirational portfolio review rather than a dated forward catalyst. Net: a genuine structural story but the unpriced, dated forward leg required to fire ACTIVE is absent; demote to WATCH pending a named/dated second divestiture or an SOP figure at Q2 (~Aug 2026).
primary_source: Ziff Davis 8-K / press release "Definitive Agreement to Sell Connectivity Division to Accenture for $1.2B" (Mar 3, 2026); Q1 2026 results 8-K deferring FY2026 guidance (reported May 7, 2026); SEC EDGAR CIK 0001084048.
